issue.message: Avoid hardcoding the debug mode; leaving it out allows debug and release builds to automatically assign one
issue.summary: Hardcoded value of android:debuggable in the manifest
issue.explanation: Hardcoding android:debuggable can cause security problems (debuggable apps allow full access via adb). Remove the attribute and let the build system set it.

issue.message: On SDK version 23 and below, your app data will be automatically backed up to Google Drive; consider android:allowBackup="false" to opt out
issue.explanation: The android:allowBackup attribute determines if an application's data can be backed up and restored, as documented here.

issue.message: Exported receiver does not require permission
issue.summary: Exported receiver does not require permission
issue.explanation: Exported services, receivers, providers, or activities should either be protected with a permission or explicitly marked as not exported.

issue.message: Using setJavaScriptEnabled can introduce XSS vulnerabilities into your application
issue.explanation: Your code should not invoke setJavaScriptEnabled if you are loading content from any source that is not fully trusted.
